speaker: LORENZO
text: Madam, although I speak it in your presence,

speaker: LORENZO
text: You have a noble and a true conceit

speaker: LORENZO
text: Of godlike amity, which appears most strongly

speaker: LORENZO
text: In bearing thus the absence of your lord.

speaker: LORENZO
text: But if you knew to whom you show this honor,

speaker: LORENZO
text: How true a gentleman you send relief,

speaker: LORENZO
text: How dear a lover of my lord your husband,

speaker: LORENZO
text: I know you would be prouder of the work

speaker: LORENZO
text: Than customary bounty can enforce you.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I never did repent for doing good,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nor shall not now; for in companions

speaker: PORTIA
text: That do converse and waste the time together,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Whose souls do bear an equal yoke of love,

speaker: PORTIA
text: There must be needs a like proportion

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of lineaments, of manners, and of spirit;

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which makes me think that this Antonio,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Being the bosom lover of my lord,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Must needs be like my lord. If it be so,

speaker: PORTIA
text: How little is the cost I have bestowed

speaker: PORTIA
text: In purchasing the semblance of my soul

speaker: PORTIA
text: From out the state of hellish cruelty!

speaker: PORTIA
text: This comes too near the praising of myself;

speaker: PORTIA
text: Therefore no more of it. Hear other things:

speaker: PORTIA
text: Lorenzo, I commit into your hands

speaker: PORTIA
text: The husbandry and manage of my house

speaker: PORTIA
text: Until my lord’s return. For mine own part,

speaker: PORTIA
text: I have toward heaven breathed a secret vow

speaker: PORTIA
text: To live in prayer and contemplation,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Only attended by Nerissa here,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Until her husband and my lord’s return.

speaker: PORTIA
text: There is a monastery two miles off,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And there we will abide. I do desire you

speaker: PORTIA
text: Not to deny this imposition,

speaker: PORTIA
text: The which my love and some necessity

speaker: PORTIA
text: Now lays upon you.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Madam, with all my heart.

speaker: LORENZO
text: I shall obey you in all fair commands.

speaker: PORTIA
text: My people do already know my mind

speaker: PORTIA
text: And will acknowledge you and Jessica

speaker: PORTIA
text: In place of Lord Bassanio and myself.

speaker: PORTIA
text: So fare you well till we shall meet again.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Fair thoughts and happy hours attend on you!

speaker: JESSICA
text: I wish your Ladyship all heart’s content.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I thank you for your wish, and am well pleased

speaker: PORTIA
text: To wish it back on you. Fare you well, Jessica.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Now, Balthazar,

speaker: PORTIA
text: As I have ever found thee honest true,

speaker: PORTIA
text: So let me find thee still: take this same letter,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And use thou all th’ endeavor of a man

speaker: PORTIA
text: In speed to Padua. See thou render this

speaker: PORTIA
text: Into my cousin’s hands, Doctor Bellario.

speaker: PORTIA
text: And look what notes and garments he doth give thee,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Bring them, I pray thee, with imagined speed

speaker: PORTIA
text: Unto the traject, to the common ferry

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which trades to Venice. Waste no time in words,

speaker: PORTIA
text: But get thee gone. I shall be there before thee.

speaker: BALTHAZAR
text: Madam, I go with all convenient speed.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Come on, Nerissa, I have work in hand

speaker: PORTIA
text: That you yet know not of. We’ll see our husbands

speaker: PORTIA
text: Before they think of us.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Shall they see us?

speaker: PORTIA
text: They shall, Nerissa, but in such a habit

speaker: PORTIA
text: That they shall think we are accomplishèd

speaker: PORTIA
text: With that we lack. I’ll hold thee any wager,

speaker: PORTIA
text: When we are both accoutered like young men,

speaker: PORTIA
text: I’ll prove the prettier fellow of the two,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And wear my dagger with the braver grace,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And speak between the change of man and boy

speaker: PORTIA
text: With a reed voice, and turn two mincing steps

speaker: PORTIA
text: Into a manly stride, and speak of frays

speaker: PORTIA
text: Like a fine bragging youth, and tell quaint lies

speaker: PORTIA
text: How honorable ladies sought my love,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which I denying, they fell sick and died—

speaker: PORTIA
text: I could not do withal!—then I’ll repent,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And wish, for all that, that I had not killed them.

speaker: PORTIA
text: And twenty of these puny lies I’ll tell,

speaker: PORTIA
text: That men shall swear I have discontinued school

speaker: PORTIA
text: Above a twelvemonth. I have within my mind

speaker: PORTIA
text: A thousand raw tricks of these bragging jacks

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which I will practice.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Why, shall we turn to men?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Fie, what a question’s that,

speaker: PORTIA
text: If thou wert near a lewd interpreter!

speaker: PORTIA
text: But come, I’ll tell thee all my whole device

speaker: PORTIA
text: When I am in my coach, which stays for us

speaker: PORTIA
text: At the park gate; and therefore haste away,

speaker: PORTIA
text: For we must measure twenty miles today.
